title: Ingress, Gateway API 1.5.1, Routing, TLS, Delegation, and Status · Reconciliation Brief: K8s Referencegrant Status Conformance
summary: Platform and application teams must share external HTTP routing without annotation-specific coupling, cross-namespace privilege, stale status, or unverified TLS ownership. Planned artifact: Add and verify the k8s referencegrant status conformance increment in Gateway API routing and delegation policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Authorize cross-namespace references with ReferenceGrant and verify Accepted, Programmed, ResolvedRefs, observedGeneration, attached routes, and implementation conformance.
Compose HTTPRoute parent references, hostnames, path and header matches, precedence, filters, weighted backends, timeouts, retries, and backend TLS policy.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never invoke kubectl, read kubeconfig, contact a cluster API, or execute host commands.
workspace: kubernetes
version-baseline:
  kubernetes: 1.36.2
  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example Gateway API 1.5.1 route is not Accepted, a cross-namespace backend lacks permission, two rules conflict, and TLS terminates with the wrong certificate. Establish controller support, attachment, ReferenceGrant, matching, status, and conformance evidence. Competency probe: Authorize cross-namespace references with ReferenceGrant and verify Accepted, Programmed, ResolvedRefs, observedGeneration, attached routes, and implementation conformance. A defensible solution applies K8s Referencegrant Status Conformance, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to Gateway API routing and delegation policy.

content.code:
# Evidence: k8s-worked-k8s-gateway-ingress-routing-tls-4
  - competency: k8s-worked-k8s-gateway-ingress-routing-tls-4
    artifact: gateway-route.yaml
    decision: worked k8s gateway ingress routing tls 4
    environment: air-gapped release cluster using an internal registry mirror
    fault-injection: drop one metrics series during an autoscaling surge
    retained-proof: accessible incident timeline with causal regression evidence
    case-key: worked4
    change: |
      gatewayClassName: conformant-public
      listener: https-status-example-test
      tls-certificate: platform/status-cert
      route: intake/status-route
      backend: intake/status-api:8080
      referenceGrant: platform-to-intake
      status: Accepted-Programmed-ResolvedRefs
    verify: kubectl get gateway,httproute,referencegrant -A -o json
    failure: Reject controller-free Gateway assumptions, cross-namespace references without grants, rule-order myths, stale status, or unverified TLS ownership.
    repair: Select a conformant implementation, bind listeners and Routes, authorize references, inspect observedGeneration conditions, and transfer-test traffic.
content.caption: Runnable Kubernetes object and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.
